English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Anthony Caputi papers
Caputi, Anthony, 1924-2008.
14-12-3593
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1956-1989 Correspondence, Printed Materials Includes lecture notes, correspondence, and course material on various drama courses. taught. Collection material in English 6057889
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Meaghan Corbett
Date completed:
June 2007
EAD encoding:
Meaghan Corbett, June 2007
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of English and Comparative Literature, Cornell University from 1956-1991, serving some time as chair in both departments.
He completed his B.A. in English at the University of Buffalo, followed by his M.A. in 1951. In 1956 he received his PhD from Cornell. He received a Fulbright Fellowship at Oxford University in 1954-1955, a senior Fulbright Fellowship, along with a Guggenheim Fellowship to Italy in 1964-1965 and in 1971-1972 he held a National Endowment for the Humanities Fellowship.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes lecture notes, correspondence, and course material on various drama courses. taught.
Cite As:
Anthony Caputi papers, #14/12/3593. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Drama -- Study and teaching. College teachers. English literature -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. English Department Cornell University -- : Faculty.
